name: Decision Matrix
visual_prompt: A 4x3 grid with cells filled at varying intensities, one column glowing brighter than the rest — the winner column. Weighted comparison made visible through graduated shading.

name: Impact-Effort Matrix
visual_prompt: A 2x2 grid with axes labeled by arrows; the top-left quadrant filled with a bold star or checkmark while the bottom-right quadrant is crossed out. Priority made spatial.

name: OODA Loop
visual_prompt: Four labeled circles — Observe, Orient, Decide, Act — connected by curved arrows forming a tight clockwise cycle, with motion lines suggesting rapid rotation. Speed encoded in the arc.

name: Cynefin Framework
visual_prompt: A rounded square divided into four unequal quadrants with a fifth irregular void at the center, each region shaded a distinct tone. One quadrant has a jagged boundary to signal disorder.

name: Hard Choice Model
visual_prompt: A 2x2 grid with one quadrant spotlit while the others are dim — the spotlight falls on the high-impact, low-comparability corner. A compass needle points directly at the lit quadrant.

name: Second-Order Thinking
visual_prompt: A single arrow triggering a chain of smaller arrows that cascade in a branching tree, each generation slightly smaller and further from the origin. Downstream consequences radiating from one action.

name: Inversion
visual_prompt: An arrow aimed forward at a goal, then curving back as a dotted return path under the same arc — the reverse route traced beneath the original. Backward thinking visualized as a mirror trajectory.

name: Pre-Mortem
visual_prompt: A calendar page with a bold X marked on a future date, and a thought bubble emerging backward in time toward the present showing cracked or broken pieces. Imagined failure projected into the past.

name: Eisenhower Matrix
visual_prompt: A crisp 2x2 grid with axes for Urgent and Important; the top-right quadrant has a lightning bolt, bottom-left a trash icon, top-left a clock, and bottom-right a calendar. Priority categories made iconic.

name: Six Thinking Hats
visual_prompt: Six hats arranged in a circle, each a distinct color — white, red, black, yellow, green, blue — with all pointing inward as if worn simultaneously by the same head. Parallel perspectives unified.

name: Veil of Ignorance
visual_prompt: A translucent curtain or veil suspended before a set of silhouetted figures in different postures — some tall, some small, one with a cane — the designer's figure hidden behind the veil. Fairness by anonymity.

name: Regret Minimization Framework
visual_prompt: An elderly figure sitting in a rocking chair, a thought bubble above showing a forked road with one path taken and one path fading — the untaken path dimming into fog. Looking back from 80.

name: Reversible vs. Irreversible Decisions (Type 1 / Type 2)
visual_prompt: Two doors side by side: one a revolving door (two-way, easy return) and one a vault door sealed with bolts (one-way, permanent). Door type encodes decision weight.

name: Expected Value
visual_prompt: A probability tree branching into two outcomes — one branch thick and short (high probability, small payoff) and one thin and long (low probability, large payoff) — with a weighted midpoint marker. Risk and reward balanced on a scale.

name: Opportunity Cost
visual_prompt: A hand reaching for one coin while a larger pile of coins sits in shadow just out of reach — the foregone alternative dimly visible behind the chosen one. The cost of the unchosen path.

name: Sunk Cost Awareness
visual_prompt: A hole in the ground with a shovel beside it and coins disappearing into the dark below — a figure stepping away instead of continuing to dig. Sunk investment as an irretrievable pit.

name: Margin of Safety
visual_prompt: A bridge designed to hold ten weights but only carrying three — the gap between load and capacity shown as a highlighted buffer zone. Safety encoded as visible slack between limit and use.

name: Occam's Razor
visual_prompt: A straight razor slicing through a tangle of twisted lines, leaving a single clean straight path behind. Simplicity cut from complexity with one decisive stroke.

name: Hanlon's Razor
visual_prompt: Two speech bubbles side by side — one with a skull (malice) crossed out with a red X, and one with a shrug or question mark (incompetence) circled in green. Benign explanation chosen over hostile.

name: Probabilistic Thinking
visual_prompt: A fan of overlapping translucent arrows spreading from a single origin point, each at a different angle and thickness representing different probability weights. Uncertainty as a spectrum of possible futures.

name: Bayesian Updating
visual_prompt: A balance scale where one pan holds a prior probability bar and new evidence chips fall into the other pan, tilting the scale to a revised position. Belief calibration made tangible.

name: First Principles Thinking
visual_prompt: A complex structure deconstructed into basic geometric shapes — cube, sphere, cylinder — laid out as building blocks at the base. Reduction to irreducible truths.

name: Circle of Competence
visual_prompt: A bold circle with a solid interior zone and a clearly marked dashed boundary ring — inside is sharp and confident, outside fades to gray uncertainty. Know the edge.

name: Map Is Not the Territory
visual_prompt: A folded paper map overlaid on a landscape it only partially matches — key terrain features exist on the ground but are missing from the map. Representation versus reality.

name: Ladder of Inference
visual_prompt: A vertical ladder with seven rungs, the bottom rung labeled with raw data dots and the top rung showing a bold action arrow — intermediate rungs show progressive abstraction steps ascending upward.

name: Pareto Principle (80/20 Rule)
visual_prompt: A bar chart where one tall bar (20%) towers over four short bars (80%), with a dividing line showing disproportionate impact. Vital few versus trivial many.

name: 10-10-10 Rule
visual_prompt: Three concentric clock faces nested together — innermost tiny, middle medium, outermost large — representing minutes, months, and years expanding outward from a single decision point.

name: WRAP Framework
visual_prompt: Four shield quadrants arranged in a 2x2 grid, each containing a distinct symbol: wide arrows for options, a lens for reality-testing, a distance marker for perspective, and a warning triangle for being wrong. Four defenses unified.

name: Confidence Determines Speed vs. Quality
visual_prompt: A diagonal axis with a speedometer needle on one end and a quality diamond on the other, with a slider bar showing the confidence-driven trade-off between the two poles.

name: Steel-Manning
visual_prompt: A muscular steel arch — a structural bridge shape — framing an opposing arrow, suggesting that the opposing view is being strengthened and held up rather than knocked down. Fortified argument.

name: Goodhart's Law
visual_prompt: A target bullseye with an arrow hitting the center ring perfectly, but the underlying goal marker has drifted off to the side — the measure is hit, the goal is missed. Metric divorced from meaning.

name: Reference Class Forecasting
visual_prompt: A bell curve distribution of past project outcomes with a single optimistic point estimate falling far to the left of the true peak — the outside view anchor correcting the inside-view outlier.

name: Confirmation Bias
visual_prompt: A magnifying glass that only shows matching puzzle pieces, while mismatching pieces outside the lens are faded out. Selective amplification of confirming evidence.

name: Loss Aversion
visual_prompt: An asymmetric scale where a small loss weight on one side drops much lower than a larger gain weight on the other — the emotional gravity of loss exaggerated by tilted proportions.

name: Parkinson's Law
visual_prompt: A fluid blob expanding to fill a rectangular container exactly — the blob shapeless and spreading outward to touch every wall, no matter the container size. Work inflating to fill available space.

name: Scenario Planning
visual_prompt: A single path that forks into three distinct branching trajectories — one curving up, one flat, one descending — each leading to a different horizon flag. Multiple futures mapped from one present.

name: Decision Tree
visual_prompt: A branching tree structure with probability-labeled forks and payoff values at leaf nodes, one highlighted path tracing the maximum expected value route from root to outcome.

name: Abstraction Laddering
visual_prompt: A vertical ladder with an upward arrow labeled 'Why?' ascending toward a wide cloud shape, and a downward arrow labeled 'How?' descending toward concrete tool icons — moving between purpose and implementation.

name: Issue Trees (Logic Trees)
visual_prompt: A MECE tree diagram branching symmetrically from one root node into non-overlapping sub-branches, with clean gaps between sibling nodes showing mutual exclusivity and complete coverage.

name: MECE Principle
visual_prompt: A rectangle perfectly divided into non-overlapping, gap-free sections of different sizes — each slice distinct, all slices together covering the whole. Complete coverage with zero overlap.

name: Five Whys
visual_prompt: A vertical chain of five links, each labeled with a progressively smaller arrow pointing downward, ending at a glowing root node at the bottom. Depth revealed through descent.

name: Fishbone Diagram (Ishikawa / Cause-and-Effect Diagram)
visual_prompt: A horizontal spine with diagonal bones branching off each side, converging at a single problem box on the right — a fish skeleton seen from above. Cause clusters feeding one effect.

name: TRIZ (Theory of Inventive Problem Solving)
visual_prompt: Two opposing arrows locked in a head-on collision forming an X, with a curved solution arc bridging cleanly over both. Contradiction resolved by going above it.

name: SCAMPER
visual_prompt: A single object — a simple cube — shown seven times in a radial arrangement, each copy transformed differently: rotated, split, shrunk, merged, flipped. Systematic variation on one original.

name: Lateral Thinking
visual_prompt: A straight downward arrow drilling into the ground beside it, while a curved dotted arrow loops sideways around an obstacle to reach the same destination. Detour as the smarter path.

name: Morphological Analysis (Zwicky Box)
visual_prompt: A 3x4 grid matrix with each column representing a dimension and each row a variation; one diagonal path highlighted across columns selects one option per dimension. The search space made exhaustive.

name: Design Thinking
visual_prompt: Five concentric arcs forming a stylized eye shape — empathy, define, ideate, prototype, test — each arc a different shade, orbiting a human silhouette at the center.

name: Double Diamond
visual_prompt: Two diamond shapes placed end-to-end: the first expands then narrows to define the right problem, the second expands then narrows to deliver the right solution. Diverge-converge repeated twice.

name: Divergent / Convergent Thinking
visual_prompt: A single point that fans out into many radiating arrows on the left (diverge), then those arrows funnel back into a single point on the right (converge). Expansion and contraction as one symmetric motion.

name: Creative Problem Solving (CPS) Model
visual_prompt: A winding path with alternating wide loops (diverge) and tight pinch-points (converge) repeating three times before reaching a destination flag. Structured oscillation leading to a solution.

name: Root Cause Analysis
visual_prompt: A tree shown upside down: the visible symptom sits at the top as a leaf, branches trace downward through layers of soil to a single taproot glowing at the bottom. The visible caused by the buried.

name: Concept Fan
visual_prompt: A folding hand fan fully spread open, each rib a different solution branch radiating from a single pivot point that represents the problem restated at broader levels. Wider framing, more options.

name: Mind Mapping
visual_prompt: A central circle with organic curved branches radiating outward in all directions, each branch splitting into smaller sub-branches like a neural tree. Associative structure made visible.

name: Synectics
visual_prompt: Two dissimilar objects — a gear and a leaf — connected by a bridge arrow, with a glowing lightbulb emerging at the bridge midpoint. Distant domains colliding to spark insight.

name: Analogical Thinking
visual_prompt: Two distinct shapes — a bridge arch and an unfamiliar gap — connected by a dotted transfer arrow, showing the arch structure copied and placed over the gap as a solution scaffold.

name: Constraint Relaxation
visual_prompt: A tight rectangular box with dashed walls that dissolve outward, revealing a much larger solution space beyond them. Invisible walls becoming permeable.

name: Reframing
visual_prompt: A picture frame that rotates 90 degrees, revealing the same scene transformed into a completely different composition. Same elements, new meaning through changed orientation.

name: Productive Thinking Model
visual_prompt: Six stepping stones arranged in a curved ascending path, each stone slightly higher than the last, with the final stone elevated above a clear goal marker. Structured ascent to solution.

name: Brainstorming
visual_prompt: Multiple speech bubbles of varying sizes exploding outward from a central point in all directions, each bubble distinct — no filter, no hierarchy, pure volume. Uncensored generation.

name: Retrograde Analysis (Working Backwards)
visual_prompt: A trophy or destination flag on the right with a dotted path traced backwards leftward through waypoints, each waypoint revealed by the prior one — the path found in reverse. End-first reasoning.

name: Problem Decomposition
visual_prompt: A large irregular boulder splitting into smaller geometric fragments — cube, pyramid, sphere — each a different color. One unsolvable mass becoming many tractable pieces.

name: Empathy Map
visual_prompt: A human head silhouette divided into four quadrants — Think, Feel, Say, Do — each quadrant filled with a distinct texture or pattern. A window into the inner and outer life of a person.

name: Reverse Brainstorming
visual_prompt: A downward arrow transforming into an upward arrow at a mirror line, with scattered ideas above and their inverted counterparts reflected below. Problem-as-solution flipped.

name: Ishikawa Conflict Resolution Diagram (Evaporating Cloud)
visual_prompt: Two diverging arrows (A and B) rooted in a shared hidden link — a dashed chain — that dissolves into mist. The invisible assumption evaporating to reveal a third path.

name: Delphi Method
visual_prompt: Three concentric circles converging inward through successive rounds, with scattered dots on the outer ring clustering tightly toward the center. Anonymous consensus spiraling in.

name: Reinforcing Feedback Loop
visual_prompt: A circular arrow forming a loop with a plus sign at the junction, each pass of the arrow growing slightly thicker and larger. Compounding amplification made visible.

name: Balancing Feedback Loop
visual_prompt: A pendulum swinging toward a target line, its arc diminishing with each pass until it rests at equilibrium. Self-correcting motion toward a fixed goal.

name: Stock and Flow
visual_prompt: A rectangular tank partially filled with water, a faucet arrow flowing in from the top and a drain arrow flowing out from the bottom. The bathtub metaphor for accumulation and rate.

name: Causal Loop Diagram (CLD)
visual_prompt: Four labeled nodes connected by curved arrows forming two interlocking loops — one reinforcing, one balancing — showing the web of cause and effect in a system.

name: Leverage Points
visual_prompt: A fulcrum lever where a small applied force on one side tips a massive boulder on the other — the lever placed close to the boulder end to maximize impact. Maximum effect from minimal input.

name: Iceberg Model
visual_prompt: A classic iceberg cross-section where the visible tip (events) floats above a waterline, with three progressively larger layers descending into darkness — patterns, structures, mental models.

name: Connection Circles
visual_prompt: A circle of labeled nodes around the perimeter with curved arrows crossing the interior, forming interlocking arcs that trace circular causality. A web inside a ring.

name: Limits to Growth Archetype
visual_prompt: An S-curve that rises steeply then flattens against a ceiling line — a reinforcing arrow pushing upward while a constraining wall deflects it horizontal. Growth meeting its boundary.

name: Shifting the Burden Archetype
visual_prompt: A broken pipe with a bandage patch diverting flow away from the root crack below, while the crack widens unnoticed. Symptomatic fix drawing attention from the real fracture.

name: Tragedy of the Commons
visual_prompt: A shared pasture divided into radiating wedges, each with an animal grazing — the center of the pasture depleted and bare while individual wedges are still full. Collective depletion from individual gain.

name: Fixes That Fail Archetype
visual_prompt: A quick-fix arrow that arcs over the present moment and lands back amplifying the original problem via a delayed looping path. The short-term solution boomeranging into a larger wound.

name: Escalation Archetype
visual_prompt: Two arrows pointing at each other, each one growing thicker and longer in successive steps, forming a staircase of mutual threat. An escalating standoff ratcheting upward.

name: Drifting Goals Archetype
visual_prompt: A horizontal target line that silently steps downward over time to meet a stagnant performance bar, while the gap between them stays constant. The standard sinking to the floor.

name: Success to the Successful Archetype
visual_prompt: Two scales side by side sharing a single resource pool — as one scale tip fills heavier, resources visibly drain from the lighter scale, increasing the imbalance. Winner-take-all compounding.

name: Growth and Underinvestment Archetype
visual_prompt: A rising growth curve that plateaus, then a capacity-investment arrow that shrinks just as the plateau hits, causing the curve to slope downward in a self-sealing loop. Decline justified by the very gap that demands investment.

name: Campbell's Law
visual_prompt: A ruler being bent and warped by a heavy downward pressure from an arrow above, while the measurement markings visibly distort. A clean metric corrupted by the weight of high stakes.

name: Conway's Law
visual_prompt: An org chart of boxes and lines on the left mirrored exactly by a software architecture diagram on the right — the two structures identical, joined by a reflection line down the middle.

name: Gall's Law
visual_prompt: A small seedling on the left growing through incremental rings into a towering complex tree on the right — beside it, a blueprint for a complex tree crossed out with an X.

name: Emergence
visual_prompt: Many small identical dots arranged in a loose swarm at the bottom, their collective shape forming the clear silhouette of a bird in flight at the top — whole greater than parts.

name: Complex Adaptive Systems (CAS)
visual_prompt: A web of small nodes connected by curved lines, each node pulsing with a small directional arrow showing it orienting toward neighbors — the entire network alive with distributed adaptation.

name: Wicked Problems
visual_prompt: A tangled knot where pulling any one strand tightens others — no loose end, no clear starting point, the knot itself transforming as you look at it.

name: Normal Accidents Theory
visual_prompt: Three independent chains of components, each with one broken link — the chains crossing each other at a single point where all three failures align and collide, triggering collapse.

name: Antifragility
visual_prompt: A muscle fiber shown under a heavy dumbbell load — the fiber emerging thicker and stronger on the other side of the stress, with upward arrows indicating growth through pressure.

name: Black Swan
visual_prompt: A tight bell curve of white swans with a single black swan perched far out on the extreme tail, dwarfing the entire curve in the silhouette it casts.

name: Lindy Effect
visual_prompt: A timeline where old books, columns, and symbols stand tall while newer items on the right are faded or toppled — the oldest artifacts casting the longest forward shadow.

name: Chaos Theory / Butterfly Effect
visual_prompt: A butterfly on the left with a tiny dotted line from its wing that diverges into two wildly different storm paths on the right — small cause, vast forking consequences.

name: Strange Attractor
visual_prompt: A looping, never-repeating orbital path that weaves around two focal points in a figure-eight butterfly shape — chaotic motion contained within a recognizable bounded form.

name: Power Laws / Fat Tails
visual_prompt: A distribution curve where the left spike towers extremely high and the right tail stretches far longer than expected — a fat tail extending well beyond where a normal bell curve would end.

name: Network Effects (Metcalfe's Law)
visual_prompt: A small cluster of 3 nodes with few connection lines doubling to 6 nodes with dramatically more lines, then 12 nodes with an explosion of connections — value multiplying faster than nodes.

name: Scale-Free Networks / Preferential Attachment
visual_prompt: A network where one massive hub node has dozens of connections radiating outward, surrounded by many tiny nodes with only one or two links each — a stark hub-and-spoke power hierarchy.

name: Tipping Point
visual_prompt: A ball rolling slowly along a curved surface toward a ridge, then shown in the next beat tumbling rapidly down the far slope with accelerating speed — the threshold crossed and irreversible.

name: Diffusion of Innovation (S-Curve)
visual_prompt: An S-shaped adoption curve rising from flat to steep to flat again, with five distinct shaded bands along its length — innovators thin, early majority wide, laggards tapering at the end.

name: Panarchy / Adaptive Cycle
visual_prompt: Four phases labeled around an infinity-loop figure-eight: a rising growth arc, a tight conservation peak, a collapse drop, and a spreading reorganization sweep — a continuous cycle of rise, rigidity, release, renewal.

name: Unintended Consequences
visual_prompt: A straight forward-pointing arrow labeled with intent, casting a curved shadow behind it that loops back and strikes from an unexpected angle — action and its hidden returning consequence.

name: Cobra Effect
visual_prompt: A hand releasing a caged cobra labeled 'bounty,' with many more cobras multiplying around it — the incentive that was meant to reduce snakes visibly producing more.

name: Jevons Paradox (Rebound Effect)
visual_prompt: A downward efficiency arrow on the left (small flame) paired with an upward consumption arrow on the right (large stack of flames), connected by a paradox curve. Saving less leads to using more.

name: Braess's Paradox
visual_prompt: A road network diagram where adding a central bridge (new connection) creates a bottleneck — traffic arrows converge and slow at the new link while bypassing the formerly free paths.

name: Path Dependence / Lock-In
visual_prompt: A tree of forking paths where early branches are thick and locked with a chain, funneling all routes through one narrow corridor regardless of where later forks might lead.

name: Hysteresis
visual_prompt: A loop curve that traces a different path going up than coming down — like a lopsided oval showing input on the x-axis and output on the y-axis, where history bends the return route.

name: Nonlinearity
visual_prompt: A curved S-shaped line on a graph sharply diverging from a straight diagonal baseline — a small horizontal nudge at the inflection point produces a massive vertical leap.

name: Delays
visual_prompt: An action arrow on the left and an effect arrow on the right, separated by a wide hourglass in the middle. Oscillating waves downstream show the system overshooting due to the lag.

name: Resilience
visual_prompt: A bowl with a ball inside — the ball rolls to the edge under a shockwave but returns to center, while the bowl itself slightly reshapes and widens to absorb future disturbance.

name: Self-Organization
visual_prompt: Scattered dots at the edges spontaneously forming a coherent pattern — a murmuration-like chevron — with no central node, only local arrows between neighboring dots.

name: Collective Action Problem
visual_prompt: Four individual figures each pulling a rope outward from a central shared resource (a shrinking circle), depleting it even as each arrow benefits a single actor. The commons collapses.

name: Boiling Frog
visual_prompt: A thermometer rising by tiny increments inside a container, with each small tick barely visible but the cumulative level approaching a critical threshold marked by a red line.

name: Forrester's Counterintuitive Behavior
visual_prompt: A tangled web of feedback loops where an intuitive push-down arrow (marked wrong) worsens the system, and a small counter-intuitive lever far from the problem (marked right) is the actual fix.

name: Ostrom's Design Principles
visual_prompt: A hexagonal commons (shared pool) surrounded by eight interlocking shield segments, each representing a governance rule — a community-governed boundary rather than a single top-down lock.

name: Situation-Behavior-Impact (SBI)
visual_prompt: Three clean sequential boxes connected by arrows: a clock/location pin (situation), a visible action gesture (behavior), and a ripple wave (impact) — concrete and separated, no judgment overlay.

name: Nonviolent Communication (NVC)
visual_prompt: Four concentric rings expanding outward — innermost is an eye (observation), then a heart (feeling), then an anchor (need), then an open hand (request) — empathy radiating from center.

name: Minto Pyramid Principle (BLUF)
visual_prompt: An inverted triangle with the wide flat conclusion at the top and narrowing rows of supporting arguments and evidence below — answer first, proof underneath.

name: SCQA Framework
visual_prompt: Four stacked horizontal bars in descending width: a stable wide bar (Situation), a disrupted jagged bar (Complication), a question mark bar (Question), and a bold answer bar (Answer) — narrative tension building to resolution.

name: Ethos, Pathos, Logos (Rhetorical Triangle)
visual_prompt: An equilateral triangle with a laurel crown at the top vertex (ethos), a heart at the bottom-left (pathos), and a scale at the bottom-right (logos) — three equal sides forming one persuasive whole.

name: Monroe's Motivated Sequence
visual_prompt: Five ascending steps — a spotlight (Attention), a gap/crack (Need), a key (Satisfaction), a vision lens (Visualization), a forward arrow (Action) — stairs climbing toward committed movement.

name: Hero's Journey (Monomyth)
visual_prompt: A circular arc with a figure starting at a calm plain, descending into a dark cave at the bottom, and rising back transformed on the other side — the full orbit of departure, ordeal, and return.

name: Story Spine
visual_prompt: A spine-shaped vertical line with six short horizontal ribs branching from it, each rib a story beat progressing from calm at top to conflict at center to resolution at bottom — scaffold made visible.

name: Active Listening
visual_prompt: A large ear with concentric ripple rings radiating inward toward its center, symbolizing full absorption of sound. A small pause symbol (two vertical bars) floats nearby, representing suspended judgment.

name: Socratic Method
visual_prompt: A question mark whose tail uncoils into a chain of smaller question marks, each one slightly larger than the last — a cascade of deepening inquiry leading to a single lit lightbulb at the end.

name: Johari Window
visual_prompt: A 2x2 window frame divided into four panes — one clear (open), one mirrored (blind), one frosted (hidden), one dark (unknown). Two small figures on opposite sides of the frame, one pointing through the glass.

name: Transactional Analysis (PAC Model)
visual_prompt: Three stacked circles labeled with a crown (Parent), a diamond (Adult), and a star (Child), with a crossing arrow between the top and bottom circles showing a crossed transaction. Clean vertical stack with one diagonal arrow disrupting the alignment.

name: DESC Model
visual_prompt: Four sequential horizontal bars stacked like a ladder — Describe, Express, Specify, Consequences — each slightly wider than the last, forming a controlled ascending ramp from observation to outcome.

name: I-Messages
visual_prompt: A speech bubble shaped as the letter 'I' with an emotional wave inside it — a smooth curve representing feelings — in contrast to a sharp jagged 'YOU' arrow pointing outward that is crossed out.

name: Crucial Conversations Framework
visual_prompt: Two speech bubbles overlapping in the center to form a shared pool — the intersection glowing — while flames surround the edges, representing high stakes and strong emotion contained by a shared zone.

name: Overton Window
visual_prompt: A vertical spectrum bar from 'unthinkable' at the bottom to 'policy' at the top, with a rectangular window frame highlighting a movable middle band. A small arrow pushing the window frame upward along the bar.

name: BATNA & ZOPA
visual_prompt: Two horizontal number lines facing each other with their own marked thresholds (BATNA), and a shaded overlapping zone between them — the ZOPA — lit up where both ranges intersect.

name: Principled Negotiation (Interest-Based Negotiation)
visual_prompt: Two figures each holding a position flag above their heads, but below the surface (like an iceberg) their shared interests are illustrated as a large common root system connecting both — depth versus surface.

name: Tactical Empathy (FBI Negotiation)
visual_prompt: A mirroring pair of speech bubbles — one solid, one as its reflection — with an emotion label tag pinned to the reflected bubble. The reflection slightly softer than the original, symbolizing empathic labeling.

name: Anchoring
visual_prompt: A large ship anchor dropped on a number line, its heavy chain pulling the final settlement marker strongly toward the anchor's landing point, with a small figure on the far right unable to pull the marker back.

name: Inoculation Theory
visual_prompt: A syringe injecting a small, weakened version of a sharp spiky shape (misinformation) into a shield, which then grows thicker and more solid — a vaccine metaphor for argument resistance.

name: Cialdini's 7 Principles of Influence
visual_prompt: Seven small icons arranged in a circular pattern around a central hand — a gift (reciprocity), chain link (commitment), crowd (social proof), badge (authority), heart (liking), hourglass (scarcity), interlocking rings (unity). A compact influence wheel.

name: Pre-Suasion
visual_prompt: A spotlight illuminating a cleared stage before a message arrow enters from the side — the spotlight shaping what the audience sees first, priming the ground before the idea lands.

name: Trust Equation
visual_prompt: A balance scale where three stacked coins (Credibility, Reliability, Intimacy) sit on one pan, and a single oversized 'S' for self-orientation weighs down the opposite pan. The formula expressed as a visual ratio.

name: Psychological Safety
visual_prompt: A circle of small figures each with a speech bubble raised openly, enclosed inside a soft glowing ring — the ring acting as a protective boundary separating the open inner space from a harsh external environment.

name: Radical Candor
visual_prompt: A 2x2 grid with axes 'Care Personally' (vertical) and 'Challenge Directly' (horizontal), with the top-right quadrant brightly highlighted and the other three quadrants labeled with dimmed warning tones.

name: SCARF Model
visual_prompt: A brain silhouette divided into five vertical bands each a different color — one per domain — with a reward (up arrow) or threat (down arrow) symbol inside each band, showing the neural toggle of social triggers.

name: Five Dysfunctions of a Team
visual_prompt: A pyramid with five stacked layers, each layer cracked — the bottom crack (absence of trust) causing a fracture that propagates up through each layer to the crumbling peak (inattention to results). A structural failure cascade.

name: Servant Leadership
visual_prompt: A large hand cupped beneath a small upward-growing tree, lifting it toward light. Support flowing upward, not downward.

name: Transformational Leadership (4 I's)
visual_prompt: A central flame radiating four distinct beams outward, each beam ending in a different symbol — star, lightbulb, hand, person. One spark, four transformations.

name: Situational Leadership (Hersey-Blanchard)
visual_prompt: A dial or compass with four quadrants — Directing, Coaching, Supporting, Delegating — and an arrow pointing to the appropriate quadrant based on a small readiness gauge beside it.

name: Authentic Leadership
visual_prompt: A mirror reflecting a figure identically — no distortion, no mask, no gap between the person and their reflection. Pure correspondence.

name: Stakeholder Power-Interest Matrix (Mendelow's Matrix)
visual_prompt: A 2x2 grid with power on the vertical axis and interest on the horizontal, each quadrant containing a different engagement icon — megaphone, close eye, handshake, conversation bubble.

name: SPIN Selling
visual_prompt: A spiral of four question marks — each progressively larger — coiling inward toward a glowing lightbulb at the center. Questions that excavate to insight.

name: Four Levels of Listening (Theory U)
visual_prompt: A vertical cross-section showing four descending layers — surface ripple, clear water, deeper still, and a luminous open space at the bottom. Listening as depth.

name: Survivorship Bias
visual_prompt: A spotlight illuminating a few standing figures on a stage while dozens of fallen silhouettes lie in shadow beyond the light's edge. Only the visible survivors counted.

name: Dunning-Kruger Effect
visual_prompt: A confidence curve that peaks sharply at the beginning, plummets into a valley, then rises gradually — with a tiny figure standing triumphantly on the first peak, unaware of the cliff ahead.

name: Anchoring Effect
visual_prompt: A heavy anchor dropped at the left of a number line, with a slider that cannot move far from the anchor's position despite the long available range.

name: Availability Heuristic
visual_prompt: A brain with one vivid image — a shark fin — filling its entire interior, while a dense stack of tiny data bars sits ignored outside, dwarfed by the single memorable icon.

name: Hindsight Bias
visual_prompt: A dotted timeline ending at a star outcome, with a retroactive arrow drawn back to the start labeled as 'obvious' — rewriting a winding, uncertain path into a straight inevitable line.

name: Status Quo Bias
visual_prompt: A figure standing on a platform labeled 'now,' looking at an open door, but anchored by a thick chain connecting their ankle to the platform beneath them.

name: Endowment Effect
visual_prompt: Two identical mugs on a scale — one held in an outstretched hand tipping its side of the scale heavily down, the other sitting untouched on the lighter side. Ownership inflates perceived weight.

name: Bandwagon Effect
visual_prompt: A crowd of arrows all pointing in one direction, with a single small arrow at the edge pivoting from a different direction to join the majority stream.

name: Peak-End Rule
visual_prompt: A jagged experience timeline with one tall peak spike and a distinct endpoint, connected by dotted lines to a memory icon — while the rest of the long middle line fades to near-invisible.

name: Halo Effect
visual_prompt: A glowing halo ring above a simple figure, with golden light radiating downward and tinting every attribute bar around it the same warm color regardless of actual merit.

name: Narrative Fallacy
visual_prompt: A series of random disconnected dots, with a smooth curved arrow drawn between them forcing a clean causal path — the arrow an invention, the dots merely scattered.

name: Recency Bias
visual_prompt: A timeline graph where the rightmost recent bar is vastly oversized and brightly lit, while the equally tall historical bars recede into gray and shrink with distance.

name: Framing Effect
visual_prompt: Two identical half-filled glasses side by side — one with an arrow pointing to the empty half labeled with a frown, one with an arrow pointing to the full half labeled with a smile. Same reality, opposite frames.

name: Sunk Cost Fallacy
visual_prompt: A person's silhouette pushing a heavy boulder uphill, with a trail of coins and clocks left behind on the slope — the path forward steeper than the descent back. Irrecoverable past weighing against rational exit.

name: Fundamental Attribution Error
visual_prompt: Two facing profile silhouettes: one labeled with a character icon (a person shape inside), the other with a storm cloud. The left figure points inward at the character, the right points outward at the cloud — asymmetric attribution made visible.

name: Overconfidence Bias
visual_prompt: A dial gauge whose needle is pinned hard to the right past the 'correct' zone, with a small actual-target marker sitting far to the left. Confidence vastly overshooting reality.

name: Gambler's Fallacy
visual_prompt: A row of five coin-flip results all showing heads, with an arrow pointing to a sixth coin — and a large erroneous 'tails due' label on it. Independent events falsely linked by a dotted chain.

name: Planning Fallacy
visual_prompt: A timeline bar split into two: a short optimistic estimate band in blue and a much longer actual-duration band in red extending far past the deadline marker. Gap between expectation and reality as a horizontal chasm.

name: Availability Cascade
visual_prompt: A single small spark igniting a chain of increasingly large megaphones, each one bigger than the last and pointing outward. Amplifying repetition turning a whisper into perceived fact.

name: Backfire Effect
visual_prompt: An arrow of evidence striking a shield, but instead of penetrating it, the shield grows thicker and a counter-arrow rebounds outward. Contradiction fortifying the wall it was meant to breach.

name: Affect Heuristic
visual_prompt: A heart icon overlaid on a balance scale, its weight tipping the pan far down on one side while a small data chart sits ignored on the lighter side. Emotion overriding evidence in the weighing.

name: Curse of Knowledge
visual_prompt: Two figures facing each other: the expert's speech bubble filled with dense interconnected nodes, the novice's receiving bubble showing only a single isolated question mark. The knowledge map that can't be unseen.

name: IKEA Effect
visual_prompt: A simple wooden chair with one leg slightly uneven, but surrounded by a golden halo glow — the creator's hand still visible in a faint outline holding a screwdriver. Imperfect self-made object radiant with overvaluation.

name: Zeigarnik Effect
visual_prompt: A row of five circles: four fully filled and dimmed, one half-filled and brightly lit with a pulsing ring. The incomplete item commanding all the attention in the sequence.

name: Ben Franklin Effect
visual_prompt: A hand passing a small gift outward, with an arrow looping back to the giver's heart — the act of giving creating affection in the giver, not the receiver. Favor as a self-directed mirror.

name: Serial Position Effect
visual_prompt: A horizontal list of seven items: the first and last glowing brightly, the middle three faded and dim. A U-shaped curve of memory plotted over the sequence.

name: False Consensus Effect
visual_prompt: A single figure standing at the center of a circle, projecting identical silhouettes outward in all directions — but outside the circle only a few actual silhouettes match, most are different. Self-projection mistaken for consensus.

name: Negativity Bias
visual_prompt: A balance scale with one black stone on the left tipping it heavily down, while three white stones on the right barely lift their side. Asymmetric weight of the negative.

name: Optimism Bias
visual_prompt: A figure standing under a personal sunshine beam while storm clouds loom overhead — the clouds have a thin dotted line curving around the figure, as if the bad weather applies to everyone else. Selective exemption from risk.

name: Authority Bias
visual_prompt: A large badge or medal at the top casting a downward beam that illuminates a speech bubble below, while an identical speech bubble beside it sits in shadow — same words, different spotlight based on credential. The credential as a light source.

name: Mere Exposure Effect
visual_prompt: Four identical simple shapes in a row, each one progressively warmer in color from cool grey to glowing gold — familiarity as a gradient from indifference to preference. Repetition as warmth.

name: Illusory Truth Effect
visual_prompt: A single statement shown three times as stacked horizontal bars, each repetition layer brighter and more solid than the one below — frequency building false solidity. A lie crystallizing through repetition.

name: Representativeness Heuristic
visual_prompt: A mental archetype silhouette (a prototype figure) with a magnifying lens in front of a crowd, highlighting only the person who matches the template while the base-rate numbers float ignored in the periphery. Pattern-match overriding probability.

name: System 1 / System 2 Thinking
visual_prompt: A brain split diagonally — the left side a lightning bolt (fast, automatic) and the right side a slow gear with teeth (deliberate, analytical). The divide is sharp, contrasting speed against precision.

name: Ingroup Bias
visual_prompt: A tight circle of identical figures clustered together facing inward, with a single figure outside the circle faded and slightly smaller. The boundary of the circle is the focal element.

name: Feynman Technique
visual_prompt: A tangled knot of complex symbols on one side, with an arrow pointing to a single clean circle with a dot — the irreducible essence. Complexity distilled into a child's drawing.

name: Spacing Effect (Spaced Repetition)
visual_prompt: Three identical small books stacked at growing intervals along a timeline, each one taller than the last to show cumulative retention. Gaps between them widen as the timeline extends.

name: Interleaving
visual_prompt: Three differently-shaped tiles — circle, square, triangle — woven into an alternating checkerboard row rather than grouped in solid blocks. Mixed pattern against the sorted version.

name: Deliberate Practice
visual_prompt: A target with concentric rings, and an arrow hitting just outside the bullseye with a curved adjustment arrow nudging the aim inward. Feedback closing the gap — not random repetition.

name: Growth Mindset
visual_prompt: A seed cracking open into a sapling, with a small upward arrow tracing the growth line. The crack in the shell is the moment of effort — potential becoming capability.

name: Bloom's Taxonomy
visual_prompt: A triangle divided into six horizontal strata, the bottom wide and light (recall), the top narrow and dark (create). Each layer progressively narrower, showing the climb from memory to mastery.

name: Kolb's Learning Cycle
visual_prompt: Four circles arranged in a square, connected by curved arrows cycling clockwise — each circle a distinct shade progressing from warm to cool. The cycle has no start or end, only motion.

name: Zone of Proximal Development (ZPD)
visual_prompt: Three concentric rings: the innermost solid (can do alone), the middle ring lightly shaded (can do with help), and the outer ring faded (beyond reach). A small upward arrow stretches into the middle ring.

name: Double-Loop Learning
visual_prompt: Two concentric feedback loops — a small inner loop correcting an action, and a larger outer loop curving back to question the rule that generated the action. The outer loop breaks the inner one's assumption.

name: Learning Pyramid / Cone of Experience
visual_prompt: An inverted pyramid with the narrow tip at top (lecture, 5% retention) widening toward the base (teaching others, 90% retention). Fill intensity darkens toward the base — depth equals engagement.

name: Ebbinghaus Forgetting Curve
visual_prompt: A steep downward curve dropping sharply from a peak, then flattening near the bottom — like a ski slope. A second dashed line shows a gentler decline after a review intervention mid-curve.

name: Retrieval Practice / Testing Effect
visual_prompt: A closed book with an upward arrow pulling a glowing fact out of it — active extraction rather than passive reading. The arrow lifts the concept visibly upward, away from the page.

name: Metacognition
visual_prompt: A mirror angled toward a head silhouette, reflecting a smaller thought-bubble back at itself. The self-reflection loop — thinking watching itself think.

name: Focused vs. Diffuse Thinking
visual_prompt: Two complementary brain states side by side: a tight beam of light on the left (focused, convergent), and a soft diffuse glow radiating in all directions on the right. The contrast between laser and lantern.

name: What? So What? Now What?
visual_prompt: Three squares arranged in a horizontal chain, each connected by a rightward arrow — the first contains a circle (event), the second a spark (insight), the third a forward arrow (action). Reflection becoming motion.

name: Munger's Latticework of Mental Models
visual_prompt: A lattice grid of intersecting lines with small different-shaped nodes at each junction — circle, triangle, square — representing models from different disciplines woven into one framework.

name: Base Rate Fallacy
visual_prompt: A large pie chart (population) in the background, nearly ignored, while a magnifying glass zooms in on a single vivid slice with bright color — the specific case overwhelming the statistical whole.

name: Conjunction Fallacy
visual_prompt: A large circle (broad event) containing a smaller, more detailed circle inside it — yet the inner circle is brighter and more visually compelling, making it feel larger than the container. Specificity creating false probability.

name: Hot-Hand Fallacy
visual_prompt: A hand gripping a basketball with a glowing streak of fire trailing behind it, while a flat probability curve below shows no upward trend. Belief in momentum clashing with random chance.

name: Impact Bias
visual_prompt: A thermometer showing a wildly tall predicted spike next to a much shorter actual outcome bar. Anticipated emotional intensity towers over the modest reality.

name: Plan Continuation Bias
visual_prompt: A straight arrow on a path charging toward a visible cliff edge, ignoring a clearly marked fork that curves safely away. Momentum overriding course correction.

name: Google Effect (Digital Amnesia)
visual_prompt: A human head in profile with an empty brain cavity, and a glowing search bar floating outside it where the memory should live. Knowledge stored outside the skull.

name: Pomodoro Technique
visual_prompt: A kitchen timer dial split into alternating solid red and white wedges — four focused sprints and short rests arranged around the clock face. Structured rhythm made visible.

name: Getting Things Done (GTD)
visual_prompt: A tangle of loose thought-bubbles funneling down into a single clean inbox tray, leaving the space above empty and clear. Mental clutter captured into a trusted system.

name: Time Blocking
visual_prompt: A day-view calendar grid with every hour filled by a solid colored block — no white space, no gaps. Every minute assigned a purpose.

name: Eat the Frog
visual_prompt: A bold frog sitting front and center at the top of a task list, circled and checked off, while the remaining smaller items wait below. The hardest thing done first.

name: Two-Minute Rule
visual_prompt: A small hourglass with just two grains of sand remaining above the neck, next to a task card being swept directly into a done pile. Tiny effort, immediate action.

name: Batching
visual_prompt: Three distinct piles of identical items grouped by type — envelopes in one stack, gears in another, phone icons in a third — each processed as a single block. Like tasks clustered together.

name: Deep Work
visual_prompt: A lone figure submerged beneath a calm water surface, completely isolated from the noise and ripples above. Undisturbed depth below a distracted surface.

name: Flow State
visual_prompt: A smooth curve rising through a sweet-spot corridor between two labeled zones — boredom below and anxiety above — with a glowing point riding exactly along the optimal channel. Challenge and skill in perfect balance.

name: SMART Goals
visual_prompt: A blurry circle sharpening into a crisp bullseye target as five labeled tick marks (S, M, A, R, T) along a lens ring snap into focus. Vague intention refined to a precise mark.

name: OKRs (Objectives and Key Results)
visual_prompt: A large directional compass needle pointing to an objective at the top, with three smaller ruler-marked bars beneath it showing measurable progress increments. Direction paired with proof.

name: North Star Metric
visual_prompt: A single bright star centered above a cluster of dimmer scattered stars, with all arrows on the chart below converging toward it. One guiding light pulling all vectors.

name: Porter's Five Forces
visual_prompt: A central circle labeled 'rivalry' surrounded by four bold arrows pressing inward from the top, bottom, left, and right — each representing a competitive force. Structural pressure converging from every direction.

name: SWOT Analysis
visual_prompt: A clean 2x2 grid divided into four quadrants — two shaded on the left (internal) and two open on the right (external) — with plus signs above and minus signs below. Strategic inventory mapped on a single square.

name: Blue Ocean Strategy
visual_prompt: A crowded red sea of overlapping fish giving way to a wide open calm blue expanse on the right, with a single boat sailing into the uncontested space. Escaping bloody competition into clear waters.

name: BCG Matrix (Growth-Share Matrix)
visual_prompt: A 2x2 grid with circles of varying sizes in each quadrant — a star top-left, a cow bottom-left, a question mark top-right, a small dog bottom-right. Portfolio positions mapped by growth and share.

name: Jobs to Be Done (JTBD)
visual_prompt: A drill bit next to a clean hole in a wall — the tool set aside, the hole (the actual job) illuminated. The purchased object and the real purpose it served, separated.

name: Lean Canvas
visual_prompt: A compact single-page grid divided into nine labeled cells, with one central cell highlighted as the core problem. A lean blueprint replacing a thick stack of documents.

name: Minimum Viable Product (MVP)
visual_prompt: A simple skateboard beside a ghosted luxury car outline — the skateboard is solid and complete, the car barely sketched. Smallest complete thing versus the full vision.

name: Build-Measure-Learn
visual_prompt: Three icons — a wrench, a ruler, and a lightbulb — connected by curved arrows cycling clockwise into a tight loop. Each node feeds the next in continuous rotation.

name: Flywheel Effect
visual_prompt: A heavy circular wheel with a single arrow pushing it at the rim, momentum lines streaking behind it as it gains speed. Slow start, self-sustaining spin.

name: Compounding
visual_prompt: A line graph curving exponentially upward — nearly flat at the left, then sweeping sharply skyward at the right. The dramatic curve of time multiplying small gains.

name: Theory of Constraints (TOC)
visual_prompt: A pipeline with several wide segments and one dramatically narrow pinch point in the middle, flow accumulating before the bottleneck. One constraint governs all throughput.

name: Bottleneck Analysis
visual_prompt: A series of parallel lanes all converging into a single narrow funnel neck, with pressure arrows pushing from above. One constriction point controlling the entire flow.

name: Critical Path Method (CPM)
visual_prompt: A network of task nodes connected by arrows, with one chain highlighted in bold from start to finish while other paths are faded. The longest chain glows as the true constraint.

name: PDCA Cycle (Plan-Do-Check-Act)
visual_prompt: Four quadrant segments of a circle — each labeled with an icon: pencil, gear, magnifier, arrow — rotating clockwise as a continuous wheel. Structured iteration made visible.

name: Wardley Mapping
visual_prompt: A 2D grid with an x-axis from genesis to commodity and a y-axis of visibility, with components plotted as dots connected by a value chain. Strategic landscape as terrain map.

name: Ansoff Matrix
visual_prompt: A 2x2 grid with axes labeled Products and Markets, each quadrant shaded in progressively darker tones from top-left (safe) to bottom-right (risky). Risk gradient across growth choices.

name: McKinsey 7S Framework
visual_prompt: Seven circles arranged in a hexagonal honeycomb with one central circle, all connected by lines — a tightly interlocked molecular structure. Change one node, all others shift.

name: PESTLE Analysis
visual_prompt: Six equal-sized tiles arranged in a 2x3 grid, each with a distinct symbol — a flag, a coin, a person, a circuit, a gavel, a leaf. Environmental forces as a scanning radar grid.

name: Value Chain Analysis
visual_prompt: A horizontal chain of linked blocks flowing left to right, with one link highlighted in gold to mark where competitive advantage is built. Value accumulating link by link.

name: Theory of Constraints — Drum-Buffer-Rope
visual_prompt: A drumbeat symbol at center, a buffer stock of circles before it, and a rope tied to the entry point of a pipeline — all three elements connected in a single production flow. Constraint-paced rhythm.

name: Hedgehog Concept
visual_prompt: Three overlapping circles forming a Venn diagram — passion, best-at, and economic engine — with a single bright intersection dot at the center. Clarity emerging from triple overlap.

name: Kanban
visual_prompt: A three-column board — To Do, In Progress, Done — with colored cards visible in each column and a narrow WIP limit bar capping the middle column. Flow made visible with explicit constraints.

name: Agile / Scrum
visual_prompt: A short curved sprint arc with a flag at the end, followed by another arc beginning again — a series of small looping dashes progressing forward. Iterative momentum in short bursts.

name: PDSA Cycle (Plan-Do-Study-Act)
visual_prompt: A four-phase circular wheel identical to PDCA but with a microscope icon replacing the checkmark in the third quadrant — emphasizing deep study over simple verification.

name: Unit Economics
visual_prompt: A single customer silhouette with two vertical bars beside it — one for revenue, one for cost — showing the margin gap at the individual unit level. Profitability zoomed to one.

name: Network Effects
visual_prompt: A small cluster of three nodes connected by lines that rapidly expands outward — each new node doubles the visible connections, forming a dense web. The outer nodes glow brighter than the center, showing value growing at the edges.

name: Disruption Theory (Disruptive Innovation)
visual_prompt: A small wedge-shaped arrow entering from the bottom-left of a large established block, angling upward and through it. The incumbent block cracks from below as the smaller shape rises past it.

name: Technology Adoption Lifecycle (Crossing the Chasm)
visual_prompt: A bell curve split by a visible gap — a broken chasm — in the early segment, with a small figure mid-leap across the divide. The left peak is labeled with early adopters, the right mass is the unreached majority.

name: Competitive Moat
visual_prompt: A castle or fortress surrounded by a wide water-filled moat, viewed from above. A single bridge is the only crossing point, emphasizing the structural barrier encircling a protected inner position.

name: Pre-mortem Analysis
visual_prompt: A timeline arrow pointing right toward a project goal, but above it a ghosted cracked version of the same arrow runs in reverse — dotted, broken — tracing backward from failure to origin. Past and imagined future collapse into a single diagnostic path.

name: Law of Diminishing Returns
visual_prompt: A curve that rises steeply at first then flattens to near-horizontal, with evenly spaced vertical input bars below it — each bar the same width but the gained height between them shrinks to almost nothing. The inflection point is marked with a subtle dot.

name: Platform Business Model
visual_prompt: Two groups of figures on opposite sides connected through a central hub or bridge platform beneath them — arrows flow in both directions across the platform. The platform sits lower, supporting the exchange without being either endpoint.
